programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and supposedly Jewish jewelers fight to track down a priceless stolen diamond.

programme.title: Abraham Lincoln: Vampire Hunter
programme.desc: Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States, discovers vampires are planning to take over the United States. He makes it his mission to eliminate them.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 4
programme.desc: John Wick uncovers a path to defeating The High Table. But before he can earn his freedom, Wick must face off against a new enemy with powerful alliances across the globe and forces that turn old friends into foes.

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
programme.desc: After stealing a mysterious orb in the far reaches of outer space, Peter Quill from Earth is now the main target of a manhunt led by the villain known as Ronan the Accuser. To help fight Ronan and his team and save the galaxy from his power, Quill creates a team of space heroes known as the "Guardians of the Galaxy" to save the galaxy.

programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Big Trouble in Little China
programme.desc: Truck driver Jack Burton gets embroiled in a supernatural battle when his best friend Wang Chi's green-eyed fiancée is kidnapped by henchmen of the sorcerer Lo Pan, who must marry a girl with green eyes in order to return to the human realm.

programme.title: Nobody
programme.desc: Hutch Mansell, a suburban dad, overlooked husband, nothing neighbor — a "nobody." When two thieves break into his home one night, Hutch's unknown long-simmering rage is ignited and propels him on a brutal path that will uncover dark secrets he fought to leave behind.

programme.title: Léon: The Professional
programme.desc: After her father, step-mother, step-sister and little brother are killed by her father's employers, the 12-year-old daughter of an abject drug dealer manages to take refuge in the apartment of a professional hitman who at her request teaches her the methods of his job so she can take her revenge on the corrupt DEA agent who ruined her life by killing her beloved brother.

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
programme.desc: After being snatched from the Green Place of Many Mothers, while the tyrants Dementus and Immortan Joe fight for power and control, the young Furiosa must survive many trials as she puts together the means to find her way home.

programme.title: Aliens
programme.desc: Ripley, the sole survivor of the Nostromo's deadly encounter with the monstrous Alien, returns to Earth after drifting through space in hypersleep for 57 years. Although her story is initially met with skepticism, she agrees to accompany a team of Colonial Marines back to LV-426.

programme.title: Ballerina
programme.desc: An assassin trained in the traditions of the Ruska Roma organization sets out to seek revenge after her father's death.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: William Munny is a retired, once-ruthless killer turned gentle widower and hog farmer. To help support his two motherless children, he accepts one last bounty-hunter mission to find the men who brutalized a prostitute. Joined by his former partner and a cocky greenhorn, he takes on a corrupt sheriff.

programme.title: WCW Main Event - 6x07 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x08 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x09 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x10 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x11 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x12 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x13 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x14 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x15 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x16 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x17 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x18 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x19 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x20 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x21 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x22 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x23 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x24 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x25 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x26 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x27 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x28 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x29 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x30 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x31 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x32 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x33 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x34 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x35 - WCW Main Event
WCW Main Event - 6x36 - WCW Main Event
Billy Madison
programme.desc: In order to inherit his fed-up father Brian's hotel empire, immature and lazy Billy Madison must repeat grades one through twelve all over again. The further Billy progresses, the harder his hated rival Eric Gordon tries to stop him.

programme.title: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: A town where everyone seems to be named Johnson is in the way of the railroad. In order to grab their land, Hedley Lemar, a politically connected nasty person, sends in his henchmen to make the town unlivable. After the sheriff is killed, the town demands a new sheriff from the Governor. Hedley convinces him to send the town its first sheriff.

programme.title: Encino Man
programme.desc: Stoney and Dave find a caveman (Link) trapped in ice, thaw him out, and show him around town. Although Link is slow to catch on to basic concepts of 20th century life, he has no trouble impressing all the girls and helping Stoney and Dave find the coolness they've been searching for.

programme.title: The Goonies
programme.desc: Young teen Mikey Walsh and his friends set off on a quest to find Pirate One-Eyed Willie's treasure in hopes of saving their homes from demolition. However, on their quest to find the treasure, they run into a family of recently escaped criminals, determined to capture the kids and reach the treasure first.

programme.title: Clue
programme.desc: Clue finds six colorful dinner guests gathered at the mansion of their host, Mr. Boddy -- who turns up dead after his secret is exposed: He was blackmailing all of them. With the killer among them, the guests and Boddy's chatty butler must suss out the culprit before the body count rises.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head Do America
programme.desc: Slacker duo Beavis and Butt-Head wake to discover their TV has been stolen. Their search for a new one takes them on a clueless adventure across America, during which they manage to accidentally become America's most wanted.

programme.title: Friday
programme.desc: A lot can happen between Thursday and Saturday - Before the sun sets, Craig must get his friend out of trouble, find a new job so his parents don't evict him, escape his girlfriend long enough to capture a new woman's heart and survive a climatic confrontation with the neighborhood menace.

programme.title: My Blue Heaven
programme.desc: An all too uptight FBI agent must protect a larger than life mobster with a heart of gold, currently under witness protection in the suburbs.

programme.title: Police Academy 5: Assignment: Miami Beach
programme.desc: Eric Lassard is about to retire, which Harris plans to exploit to take over his position. In Miami, where Lassard is to be honored, a mix-up leads to an adventure involving diamonds, kidnapping and betrayal.

programme.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
programme.desc: As a swinging fashion photographer by day and a groovy British superagent by night, Austin Powers is the '60s' most shagadelic spy. But can he stop megalomaniac Dr. Evil after the bald villain freezes himself and unthaws in the '90s? With the help of sexy sidekick Vanessa Kensington, he just might.

programme.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
programme.desc: Kevin is separated from his family again when he accidentally boards a flight to New York City during a Christmas trip to Miami. However he crosses paths with the same burglars, who now plan to rob a toy store on Christmas Eve.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: At an exclusive country club, an ambitious young caddy, Danny Noonan, eagerly pursues a caddy scholarship in hopes of attending college and, in turn, avoiding a job at the lumber yard. In order to succeed, he must first win the favour of the elitist Judge Smails, and then the caddy golf tournament which Smails sponsors.

programme.title: Napoleon Dynamite
programme.desc: Preston, Idaho's most curious resident, Napoleon Dynamite, lives with his grandma and his 32-year-old brother (who cruises chat rooms for ladies) and works to help his best friend, Pedro, snatch the Student Body President title from mean teen Summer Wheatley.

programme.title: Police Academy 6: City Under Siege
programme.desc: Our favourite police men are called together to deal with a gang who rob banks and jewelers. Using their various talents as well as their extraordinary luck, the crooks stand no chance against our men and women in blue.

programme.title: Revenge of the Nerds II: Nerds in Paradise
programme.desc: Get out your white socks! This time our suave college heroes hit the road for fun and fraternity in Florida. Another opportunity for brains to triumph over brawn. How can the girls possibly resist their geeky charms?

programme.title: Pee-wee's Big Adventure
programme.desc: The cartoonish and childish character Pee-wee Herman goes on a big adventure for the first time ever when his beloved shiny new bicycle is stolen by his nemesis Francis Buxton, a fellow man-child and neighborhood rich "kid." And he sets off on an obsessive cross-country journey, determined to recover it. Pee-wee's awkward and childish attempts to be cool and mature.

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger
programme.desc: When a downtrodden janitor, Winston Gooze, is exposed to a catastrophic toxic accident, he’s transformed into a new kind of hero: The Toxic Avenger. Now, Toxie must rise from outcast to savior, taking on ruthless corporate overlords and corrupt forces who threaten his son, his friends, and his community.

programme.title: The Wolf Man
programme.desc: Upon his return to his father's estate, aristocrat Larry Talbot meets a beautiful woman, attends a mystical carnival and uncovers a horrifying curse.

programme.title: They Live
programme.desc: Nada, a down-on-his-luck construction worker, discovers a pair of special sunglasses. Wearing them, he is able to see the world as it really is: people being bombarded by media and government with messages like "Stay Asleep," "No Imagination," "Submit to Authority." Even scarier is that he is able to see that some usually normal-looking people are in fact ugly aliens in charge of the massive campaign to keep humans subdued.

programme.title: Thir13en Ghosts
programme.desc: When Cyrus Kriticos, a very rich collector of unique things, dies, he leaves his house, fortune, and his prized collection of ghosts.

programme.title: Tremors
programme.desc: A small town gradually becomes aware of a strange creature which picks off people one by one. But what is this creature, and where is it? At the same time, a seismologist is working in the area, she detects _tremors_. The creature lives underground, and can 'pop up' without warning. Trapped in their town, the town-folk have no escape.

programme.title: Tremors II: Aftershocks
programme.desc: Earl Bassett, now a washed-up ex-celebrity, is hired by a Mexican oil company to eradicate a Graboid epidemic that's killing more people each day. However, the humans aren't the only one with a new battle plan.

programme.title: Tremors 3: Back to Perfection
programme.desc: The now famous Burt Gummer returns to his hometown of Perfection, Nevada for the first time in years, but the deadliest Graboid evolution yet forces him to save the town he swore to protect.

programme.title: Werewolf of London
programme.desc: While on a botanical expedition in Tibet Dr. Wilfred Glendon is attacked in the dark by a strange animal. Returning to London, he finds himself turning nightly into a werewolf and terrorizing the city, with the only hope for curing his affliction a rare Asian flower, the Mariphasa.

programme.title: Young Frankenstein
programme.desc: An American grandson of the infamous scientist, struggling to prove that his grandfather was not as insane as people believe, is invited to Transylvania, where he discovers the process that reanimates a dead body.

programme.title: The Old Dark House
programme.desc: Seeking shelter from a storm, five travelers are in for a bizarre and terrifying night when they stumble upon the Femm family estate.

programme.title: Killer Klowns from Outer Space
programme.desc: When a small town is invaded by aliens from outer space who are capturing and killing the townspeople, no one takes them seriously. Why? The aliens all look like circus clowns, use weapons that look clown like, and all have painted on smiles. Only a few of the young people in the town realize the danger and of course no one believes them. Armed with an ice cream truck they try and rescue their friends.

programme.title: A Bucket of Blood
programme.desc: A dimwitted busboy at a beatnik cafe passes off a cat he accidentally killed and covered in plaster as a sculpture, prompting a demand for more art that compels him to commit murders.

programme.title: Bud Abbott Lou Costello Meet the Killer Boris Karloff
programme.desc: Lost Caverns Hotel bellhop Freddie Phillips is suspected of murder. Swami Talpur tries to hypnotize Freddie into confessing, but Freddie is too stupid for the plot to work. Inspector Wellman uses Freddie to get the killer (and it isn't the Swami).

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde
programme.desc: Slim and Tubby are American cops in London to study police tactics. They wind up in jail and are bailed out by Dr. Jekyll. Jekyll has been murdering fellow doctors who laugh at his experiments. He has more murders in mind. At one point the serum that turns Jekyll into the murderous Hyde gets injected into Tubby.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein
programme.desc: The Wolf Man tries to warn a dimwitted porter that Dracula wants his brain for Frankenstein monster's body.

programme.title: Abbott and Costello Meet the Mummy
programme.desc: In Egypt Peter and Freddie find the archaeologist Dr. Zoomer murdered before they can return to America. A medallion leads them to a crypt where a revived mummy provides the terror.

programme.title: Hold That Ghost
programme.desc: Two bumbling service station attendants are left as the sole beneficiaries in a gangster's will. Their trip to claim their fortune is sidetracked when they are stranded in a haunted house along with several other strangers.

programme.title: Killers from Space
programme.desc: Atomic scientist/pilot Doug Martin is missing after his plane crashes on an reconnaissance mission after a nuclear test. Miraculously appearing unhurt at the base later, he is given sodium amethol, but authorities are skeptical of his story that he was captured by aliens determined to conquer the Earth with giant monsters and insects. Martin vows to use existing technology to destroy them.

programme.title: Aerosmith Live Osaka, Japan 1999 Millennium Countdown 2000
Paul McCartney Back In The US
Lennon Legend - The Very Best Of John Lennon
Led Zeppelin Live Royal Albert Hall
The Who - Live At The Isle Of Wight Festival (1970)
ACDC - Live at Caste Donington, England (August 17, 1991)
The Best of Ringo Starr and his All Star Band so far
Columbo
programme.desc: Columbo matches his skills against Brimmer, a former cop turned private investigator with a quick temper who tries to blackmail a client's wife. When she refuses, he accidentally kills her and it's up to Columbo to nail him.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: A major argument erupts on the day that Maude and Walter are to accept a husband and wife of the year award. The reason: Maude discovers that Walter has appointed a trustee in his will and of all people, the trustee is Arthur; Maude promptly refuses to go to the banquet to accept the award.

programme.title: Maude
programme.desc: As their impending nuptials draw near, Maude decides to give Carol and Chris their wedding gift early. They are both certainly surprised to find out that Maude has put a down payment on a house for them but the even bigger surprise is that the house is located right across the street. Immediately this sparks family tension between mother and daughter and future husband and wife.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy, Blanche and Rose each secretly pose nude for a Hungarian sculptor who will unveil the statue at an art show opening at a museum. Meanwhile, Sophia brushes up on practical jokes to get back a prankster at the Senior Citizens Center.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Blanche is thrilled by a visit from her daughter, Rebecca, an ex-model who has now gained quite a bit of weight. Also visiting with Rebecca is her oafish beau who constantly puts down her weight. The kicker: she's thinking of marrying him.

programme.title: The Golden Girls
programme.desc: Dorothy befriends a novelist, Barbara Thorndyke and she quickly infringes on Dorothy's relationship with Blanche and Rose who think Barbara is nothing but a stuck up snob.

programme.title: The Golden Palace
programme.desc: Sophia and Oliver joy ride in a guest's car and wind up in police custody.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Next door neighbor, Julio, has become a very good friend of Lamont's. With Lamont, spending so much free time with Julio, Fred begins to feel left out and decides to try to be more like one of the guys. However, Fred quickly learns the advantages of having so much free time and no one to hoard in on it.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie is invited to a meeting with some pals. THey discuss about Mike's article in the newspaper on how offending it is. The leader asks Archie if he wants to join them and give Mike and lesson he'll never forget. Archie agrees, little does he know he just initiated the KKK (Kweens Kouncil of Krusaders).

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: Archie desives a plan to prevent the Klan from burning a cross on Mike and Glorial's lawn.

programme.title: All in the Family
programme.desc: A flashback episode of when Mike and Gloria were introduced by mutual friends at the Bunker home while Archie and Edith are out. Initially their differences lead to tension until they discover a shared love for old-style dancing.

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Jennifer & Janiene vs. Carol & Pat vs. Noelle & Smitty

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.sub-title: Gayle & Marilyn vs. Deborah & Darren vs. Raquel & Andy

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.sub-title: If a Body Meets a Body
programme.desc: When Moe reads that Curly's rich uncle has died, and has left his estate to Curly, the boys go there to collect. They find that the uncle was murdered and his body and will are missing.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The Stooges are firemen who irk their captain.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are would-be reporters, who are tricked into becoming stowaways by "Borscht", a spy for an enemy country. Stranded on a freighter on the high seas, they discover that Borscht has concealed some stolen microfilm in watermelons they brought aboard for him. After a wild chase, they subdue Borscht and recover the microfilm.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges are the "Day and Night" plumbers. Called out to a fancy mansion where a society party is going on, they cross the electrical and water systems and generally ruin the place. Despite their incompetent plumbing, they save the day by recovering a painting stolen by a pair of thieves masquerading as party guests.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Larry and Shemp are living in a stolen railroad car. Larry wants to marry his girlfriend, but she won't consent until Shemp marries her sister. Shemp however, is constantly drunk and in love with "Carry", an imaginary giant canary. Moe is an investigator from the railroad, sent to discover how the car was stolen from a moving train. Moe is also in love with Shemp's girl. Shemp winds up with both women, but still prefers his imaginary canary.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges go to Egypt in search of the mummy of king Rootin-Tootin for which a museum will pay a $5000 prize. They wind up in the mummy's tomb where they are harassed by some bad guys after the same objective. The villains, who have kidnapped a professor from the museum, want the jewels buried inside the mummy. When Curly accidentally destroys the mummy, Moe and Larry wrap him in bandages to fool the bad guys. They manage to rescue the professor and retrieve the real mummy of Rootin-Tootin who turns out to have been a midget

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: The stooges run a small restaurant, and must come up with some quick money to pay off a pie dealer who's wares they ruined. They enter Curly in a milking contest at the county fair, but his technique leaves something to be desired, and he quickly falls behind the champ. Moe and try to help by putting on a cow suit and pouring milk from a concealed bottle, but when their cheating is exposed, they must leave on the run.

programme.title: Svengoolie
programme.desc: A coffin brings deadly South American spiders to California. A doctor with arachnophobia faces terror as they multiply, threatening locals.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne sends everyone to a party while she stays home -- and is visited by the ghosts of Halloween past, present, and future.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Nancy takes Roseanne and Jackie by surprise with the announcement that she and Marla are gay lovers; Bev announces she is moving into a Lanford retirement center, which leaves Roseanne feeling over the hill.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: The Lunch Box welcomes its first customers, including a weirdo whose presence sends the gals to a self-defense class. Meanwhile, Arnie questions his own manhood after hearing that Nancy is gay.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne reluctantly allows Darlene to go to a rock concert in nearby Rockford with Molly -- who abandons her.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: D.J. decides to take up hockey and becomes the terror of the rink; Roseanne meddles to reunite Jackie and Fisher.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.sub-title: It's No Place Like Home for the Holidays
programme.desc: A Christmas Eve snowstorm scatters the Connors, with Roseanne and Jackie trapped at the Luch Box with Bev and Nana Mary, while Darlene takes shelter at David's and learns the truth about his home life.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Dan disciplines D.J. for taking offensive reading material to school-- Darlene's comic book. Dan reacts strongly when Roseanne discovers that Fisher has beaten Jackie.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Part 2 of 2: Dan gets bailed out of jail by an amused Darlene, while rumors fly from wild to wilder, as to why he was arrested. Jackie learns why Dan was arrested, and finds herself both angry with, and touched by Dan's protective gesture. Overwhelmed, and feeling like things are moving too fast, Jackie leans on Roseanne to help her move out of Fisher's apartment. Roseanne lovingly declares that her family is officially "Poor White Trash".

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: With their very overdue 100 pound bonuses, the staff are eager to gain some more and bet all their money on a horse that Mr.Harman assures them will win. Watching the race on the newly installed television they all hold their breath to either win their fortunes, or lose it all.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr.Grace has allowed the ladies and gents to use their floor at night to raise extra money. They decide to hold a night club and will get the word out by advertising at the local cinema where Mr.Humphries has connections. With a borrowed camera they decide to shoot their own commercial. When it's finished they go to the cinema to view it but will it be as good as they hoped?

programme.title: Toon in With Me
programme.desc:
Bill shows off some of his Christmas collectibles on this newest edition of "Fan-Tastic Friday."
Featured cartoons:
The Year of the Mouse (1965) Naughty Neighbors (1939) A Sunbonnet Blue (1937) Canadian Can-Can (1967) Daffy Duck & Egghead (1938)

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: An art critic, Dale Kingston, kills his uncle for his valuable collection of paintings. All the clues lead to the dead man's first wife. Can Columbo correct the Lieutenant's error before it is too late?

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max is embarrassed when she discovers her Tuesday night boyfriend is a DJ at a grocery chain.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline accidentally loses Han’s expensive fish while cleaning the tank, Max considers selling a sentimental gift she just received from her mother in order to pay for a new one.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline takes an abandoned bicycle in order to start a delivery service for their cupcake business, but discovers that Max never learned how to ride one.

programme.title: WWF Prime Time Wrestling
programme.desc: "Rowdy" Roddy Piper serves as Gorilla Monsoon's new co-host for another exciting lineup of in-ring competition. However, a weasel's stench is hard to remove, as Bobby "The Brain" Heenan presents the debut edition of his very own show. Plus, Hulk Hogan & Brutus "The Barber" Beefcake answer a challenge, Mr. Perfect takes on Hercules, and much more.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: While accompanying Tina to a "Boyz 4 Now" concert, Louise develops a crush on one of the lead singers, Boo Boo - and is in disbelief that she actually likes a boy! Meanwhile, Gene gets creative - and slightly offensive - in a table-setting competition.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is upset when he finds out that Debra signed up Michael and Geoffery to play fairies in a school play.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert gets a surprise visit from Stefánia, the woman with whom he fell head over heels in love when the Barones went on holiday in Italy. Robert's excitement over having his summer love Stefania so close quickly gives way to annoyance when he is witness to a few of her idiosyncrasies. Meanwhile, Stefania's father, who has accompanied her to America, has his own change of heart with respect to his feelings for Robert.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: To Doug's delight, Carrie decides that she's ready to try for a baby again. Carrie also takes up spinning as a new hobby, and Doug is less happy about that. When Spence introduces his dog-walker friend Holly to the family, Carrie and Doug see an opportunity to re-direct her talents to their advantage.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: For Carrie's birthday, Doug decides to get her laser eye surgery. Doug brings her to the place, and the surgery is a success. The next day, Carrie wakes up to blurry vision. The next day she wakes up to the same thing. Doug calls up the doctor and asks if these symptoms are normal. The doctor says they are not normal, and Doug is now very nervous. Carrie finds out that Doug went to a cheap place, and used discounts.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Abstinence allows George to become a genius, but it turns Elaine into an idiot. Jerry gets bumped from career day at his old Jr. High School. Kramer turns his apartment into a smoking lounge and all the smoke disfigures his face.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A shipwreck survivor beats George out of an apartment, so he decides to tell the apartment board the astonishing tales of Costanza. Elaine dates a guy who has had a series of really bad break-ups. Kramer gets a nagging cough and seeks veterinary advice. Jerry learns that Newman is up for a transfer to Hawaii and tries to help him deliver mail.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne doesn't share Jackie's enthusiasm for the exciting new man in her life.

programme.title: Roseanne
programme.desc: Roseanne's meddling results in silence between the sisters, but Jackie has exciting news to share.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Karen and Jim had their first big fight in the relationship. Karen had been living in a hotel since taking her job in Scranton. When she decided to live next door to Jim, he took it as the next step to living together and that was too fast. She was not pleased with his response.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael decided to send his employees out into the field for sales calls. He paired himself up with Andy. He let the veterans choose who they wanted their partners to be. Phyllis chose Karen, Stanley chose Ryan and that left Jim and Dwight paired together. This wasn't the first time Dwight and Jim had done sales calls together.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer builds a battle robot to win Bart's respect, and places it in a tournament against other robots. What Bart does not know, though, is that the robot is actually Homer in costume. Meanwhile, Snowball II dies and Lisa tries to find a replacement cat.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge writes a romance novel with characters based on Homer and Ned Flanders that becomes a hit. Meanwhile, Homer gets fired from the power plant again and becomes an ambulance driver.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When the local library proves to be useless for the kids' class project, Marge tells Milhouse, Bart, and Lisa three Simpsons-style historical accounts: Henry VIII's (Homer's) attempts at siring a son, Lewis and Clark (Lenny and Carl) exploring the USA's wilderness with Sacagawea (Lisa), and Salieri (Lisa again) out to ruin piano-playing virtuoso Mozart (Bart).

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart finds a friend in his own sister Lisa after Milhouse moves away to Capitol City to live with his divorced mom. Meanwhile Homer goes into panhandling to buy anniversary gifts for Marge. Later on Milhouse returns and Bart goes back being friends with him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After a quarrel with Dr. Hibbert, Homer decides to enlist Maggie for a pre-nursery school. Maggie doesn't get tested because she can't talk. Back home Lisa discovers Maggie can make words and the family goes back to the pre-nursery school. This time Maggie passes the test gloriously and turns out to have an IQ of 167. This is more than Lisa's IQ (159) which makes her unsure about her identity.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Sex gets in the way of Bud studying for his finals, and he risks being expelled when he is caught in the school library.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Marcy are angry when they find Kelly and Jefferson must kiss for a coffee commercial.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Griff are listening to a sports quiz show on the radio and the winner gets an all expense paid trip to Atlanta for the 1996 Olympic games, as well as running in one of the Chicago legs of the torch carrying relay. Al as been banned from the radio show, so he feeds his answers to Griff who wins the contest for him. Al begs Griff to share the prize with him, but Griff goes back on the deal when he experiences the sudden fame he gets.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: An old friend of Peggy's comes to seduce Al and he plays along since he thinks it is one of Jefferson's practical jokes.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: The neighbors take refuge from a twister.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al and Griff blackmail Gary, because of a bad promotion contest, when they find the shoes from the shoe store are made by children in sweatshops. Meanwhile, Peggy wins a microwave and Kelly and her try to hide it from Al.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: In order to get a $20,000 contract of a model for a national olive oil brand, Kelly has to sign a Morals Clause, which requires that she remain chaste. This proves to be a difficult task. Meanwhile, Al hears that Officer Dan is appearing in Al's favorite TV show, Cops, and blackmails him to become his partner.

programme.title: Married... with Children
programme.desc: Al's Dodge starts sputtering so he sends the rest of the family to find a fuel pump for it while he recalls the good old days in his Dodge.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: After Fred gets a minor injury from a car accident, Lamont and Fred seek to find a maid to help out around the house. After looking, they get what the agency has at the bottom of the barrel. However, the erratic white woman does nothing but add to Fred's pain.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont have bills coming out of their ears and consider going on welfare, something Fred refuses to do. Fred comes up with a plan to throw a "big party" in the Sanford home and charge admission. That's when two hoods show wanting a part of the action. Meanwhile, Aunt Esther is trying to find a place to hold her weekly church group meetings.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Fred and Lamont take a tour through the NBC studios and Fred gets a kick out of seeing Johnny Carson's parking spot. However, what Fred really gets a kick out of is seeing Lena Horne's car and sneaks into her dressing room. After meeting her, he tries to trick her into visiting him at his home.

programme.title: Sanford and Son
programme.desc: Lamont goes totally African. He starts to wear African clothing, and yearns to learn more about the culture. At the same time, Fred thinks that the whole thing is ridiculous, that is until a woman from Nigeria visits and explains to Lamont how sacred listening to your father is in African culture and how you should respect him.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: After listening to Chris remind him of what a living hell his life truly is, Larry runs away, so Chris goes in search of him and thinks he found him--but it's actually just a hobo who speaks no English.

programme.title: Get a Life
programme.desc: Chris and Sharon show their true feelings when they become trapped in a meat locker together.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A squirrel in a downtown park lugs a giant coconut back home, but nothing he does can seem to crack it open.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Yosemite Sam hears that Granny has inherited fifty million dollars. Good guy Bugs tries to save Granny from Sam's clutches.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Bugs Bunny takes a wrong turn at Albuquerque and winds up in a Mexican bullring fighting one large bull.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Hypnosis doesn't help the Coyote catch the Road Runner, nor do a clutch of string-controlled rifles or dozens of mousetraps, but they all manage to backfire on him, naturally.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: The final installment of the "Hunting Trilogy" once again has Elmer out hunting, while Bugs and Daffy try to con him into shooting the other.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: Rocky the gangster kidnaps Tweety Bird for a million dollar ransom and holes up in an abandoned city building. Sylvester Cat in an alley below hears Tweety's sad cries and decides to rescue him - for his lunch.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A tiny elephant emerges from a banana boat and wanders about town, causing an uproar among the populace. Sightings are attributed variously to mass hysteria, insanity and dipsomania.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: A harried housewife describes to her boorish husband what she did that day.

programme.title: Looney Tunes
programme.desc: When Bugs calls a cab he doesn't know it's the getaway car for a couple of bankrobbers.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head get to see the film the rest of us guys never got to see in school--for educational reasons only, of course.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Mr. Anderson asks Beavis and Butt-head to go shopping for him at the drug store; they buy everything except what was on his list.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Middle-aged Beavis and Butt-Head attend a city council meeting to get their apartment demolished.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head sit in with a group for alcoholics to try to get some beer.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: After watching a horror movie, Beavis and Butt-head run around the neighborhood trying to scare people by screaming at them.

programme.title: Mike Judge's Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Butt-Head teaches Beavis a new game, which puts him in the hospital.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis & Butt-head sit in on Mr. Anderson's poker game and try to collect all the cards.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys steal Mr. Anderson's steamroller, with predictable results.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head go to the animal shelter to get a dog, and find one that really likes Beavis.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Pee-wee can't figure out what to "do" today, so Jambi gives him a to-do list which includes blowing a big balloon, dressing up as a pirate, and going for a walk with Miss Yvonne. The list also mentions cleaning up Pee-wee's room.

programme.title: Pee-wee's Playhouse
programme.desc: Miss Yvonne pays a visit to the Playhouse, yet Pee-wee is nowhere to be found. Miss Yvonne finds, instead, the words "For Sale" in front of the Playhouse abode. Shocked and confused, Miss Yvonne and everyone else try to remember all the better times that the Playhouse has gone through. Watch for today's secret "word." However, when Pee-Wee arrived to the playhouse about the "For Sale". But Pee-Wee realized that he is NOT selling the playhouse and has another sign that reads "LEMONADE". So the sign would had said. "Lemonade For Sale".

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max and Caroline's partnership - and friendship - is almost torn apart when Caroline drags Max to a seminar to help brand their cupcake business.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: After suffering a series of mysterious anxiety attacks, New Jersey mob boss Tony Soprano agrees to see a psychiatrist, Dr. Jennifer Melfi. As he opens up to the doctor, Tony reveals details about his problems at home and at work that involve his wife Carmela, his kids Meadow and Anthony Jr., his Uncle Junior and his domineering mother Livia.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: With acting boss Jackie Aprile dying of cancer, Tony prepares for a power struggle with Uncle Junior. Christopher and his associate Brendan make trouble for themselves when they jack a truck full of DVD players. Tony's mother Livia finally agrees to live in a retirement community. Big Pussy and Paulie Walnuts are sent to find a stolen car.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Eleanor puts Janet in her crosshairs to save Michael from eternal suffering.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael has a private meeting with Eleanor, who is put to the test. Also, Chidi harbors a secret and mulls over what to do.

programme.title: The Good Place
programme.desc: Michael's skills are put to the test when he attempts to sort out an unprecedented situation. Meanwhile, Chidi makes a connection elsewhere, much to the dismay of Eleanor, while Janet shows a different side of herself.

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Daemon visits his wife in the Vale. Viserys and Rhaenyra broker agreements with the Valeryons. Alicent seeks the truth about the princess.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Cersei and Tywin plot the Crown's next move. Dany discusses future plans. Jon Snow embarks on a new mission.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: The Laws of Gods and Men
programme.desc: Stannis and Davos set sail with a new strategy. Dany meets with supplicants. Tyrion faces down his father in the Throne Room.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: In the aftermath of personal tragedy, Wilson resigns from the hospital... and from his friendship with House. Meanwhile, Thirteen struggles with her personal medical problems while helping treat an executive assistant with a similar situation to her own.

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The team deals with an organ donor whose organs prove fatal, and the two surviving patients. Meanwhile, House hires a private detective to spy on Wilson, but hears a few things about himself that he'd rather not.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Feeling sorry for Ralph, Lisa sends him a Valentine card. Having developed a crush on her, Ralph invites Lisa to go see the Krusty the Clown show. She accepts but after Krusty asks them if they are boyfriend and girlfriend, Lisa lets Ralph know that she just wants them to be friends.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: With Mr. Burns shot, Chief Wiggum investigates the crime, and nearly everyone in town has a motive. At the end, it all comes down to the Simpson DNA.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: When You Dish Upon a Star
programme.desc: After a chance encounter with Hollywood celebrities secretly living in Springfield, Homer becomes their friend and gofer... until he spills the beans to the gang at Moe's.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Flanders suffers a failure after Homer wishes him ill.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: In this Christmas-themed episode, the students of Springfield Elementary are unwittingly used as a focus group to test new toys. The product of their labors, a cuddly doll named Funzo, is a lot more sinister than he appears.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family is on a nature walk through “Carl’s Dad Caverns,” when Homer tries to get a piece from a stalactite. This results in the family falling through the cave floor and winds up with Homer getting stuck in the ceiling of one of the lower chambers of the cave. Marge, Bart and Maggie go off to find help, while Lisa stays behind and tells her father a story to pass the time. Her story involves running from a bighorn sheep and into Mr. Burn’s mansion. The pair evades the tenacious sheep by finding refuge in the mansion’s attic, where Mr. Burns tells her the story of how he came to work at Moe’s Tavern by losing a scavenger hunt with a rich Texan. While there he learned (and tells to Lisa) the tale of the Moe’s treasure. The stories within stories culminate in the fate of the treasure being determined in the cave.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After tricking Marge into thinking an advertising spokesman is coming to visit her, Homer takes Marge and the rest of the family to a restaurant. A hypnotist uses his powers on Homer, and makes him remember a horrific childhood incident where Homer found a dead body in a ravine. The Simpson family decides to investigate this and find out where the body came from.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty the Clown has dinner with the Simpsons, and reveals that he is Jewish, his real name is Herschel Krustofsky, and he is estranged from his rabbi father. They parted ways over Krusty's becoming a clown instead of following his father and becoming a rabbi. Bart and Lisa see Krusty becoming depressed, and they take it upon themselves to reunite him with his father.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: She Used to Be My Girl
programme.desc: Marge's old school friend Chloe Talbot,now a famous journalist,returns to Springfield and Marge feels envious because Lisa spends so much time with her. However when Chloe and Lisa are trapped after the volcanic Mount Springfield has erupted Marge proves that she may not be famous but she can still be heroic.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe Szyslak becomes lonely and decides to commit suicide by jumping off a bridge. However, he inadvertently saves Maggie Simpson when she is sent flying from the Simpsons' car window and into his arms. He starts playing with Maggie and becomes attached to her, telling her the story of The Godfather. Homer and Marge become upset about how much time Moe is spending with Maggie and forbid him from seeing her again. One night, Maggie wakes up and hears Fat Tony and the Springfield Mafia outside the house and decides to follow them. When Homer and Marge find her missing, they automatically assume that Moe kidnapped her. When Moe is told that Maggie is missing, his request to help find her is granted by Homer. Maggie follows the mobsters to Luigi's restaurant, where Fat Tony's gang gets into a gun battle with a rival family. Homer, Marge and Moe track Maggie down and Moe decides to go inside and save Maggie. After Moe saves Maggie's life, Homer allows the two to spend more time together.

programme.title: WWE Raw
programme.desc: As Triple H & X-Pac are set to take on Test & The Rock in the main event, the New Age Outlaws are put in singles competition against the Big Boss Man & Ken Shamrock. Plus, Shane McMahon is in a giving mood, but he may go too far when he challenges Mankind to a match. Mark Henry, The Brood, Al Snow, and more are in action.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira is hired to be a local winery's spokesperson; David struggles with a health problem.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Following an incident where David is unable to kill an insect in his room, Stevie, as a joke, invites David along on Roland's annual turkey shoot.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Han emails photos of a stripper’s face to his mother claiming she’s his girlfriend, he begs Max and Caroline to find her and convince her to play his girlfriend when his mother arrives from Korea.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Caroline’s date is a no-show after promising to take her to one of the city’s hippest new restaurants, she and Max decide to take advantage of the hard-to-get reservation.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: The girls get a cleaning woman to clean the apartment and the woman unearths an application Max has for pastry school. Caroline urges to go and apply but Max doesn't think she can get in. They go and Caroline finds herself attracted to the instructor Nicolas. He asks Max to make a tart as her project to see if she can get in. Max thinks it's a piece of cake till she finds out what a tart is.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The First Day Of School
programme.desc: Max attempts to make a good impression on her first day of pastry school, but finds the class clown to be extremely distracting – and intriguing. Meanwhile, Caroline dresses to impress Max’s handsome teacher, Nicolas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline attends her former nanny’s funeral, thinking the family will be honored to have her, but quickly finds out they have no idea who she is.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: After Caroline has an incredible make-out session with Max's pastry school teacher, Chef Nicholas (recurring guest star GILLES MARINI), Caroline finds out something that makes her wish it never happened.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max finds herself wanting to be more than just friends with her pastry school partner, Deke. Meanwhile, Caroline is "hot for teacher" - the married Chef Nicholas.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke takes Max to his place after a great first date, his "home" is nothing like she expected. Meanwhile, Caroline feels empowered - then scared for her life - after having a shady car towed from the front of their apartment building.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Icing On The Cake
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max’s friends over for a gathering at his dumpster, Caroline learns an enormous secret about his background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Max decides to break up with Deke now that she knows about his wealthy background.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline asks Max and Deke to join her on her dinner date at Nicolas’s loft to help make sure she doesn’t do anything she might regret. Also, Max and Caroline treat themselves to very special hair appointments.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline goes into a tailspin and brings Max into the mix when Nicolas tells her he's going to leave his wife for her.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to fit in with Max and the diner gang as they go out to get wild and crazy at the St. Patrick's Day parade, but finds herself longing to practice her former holiday traditions at the Plaza Hotel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: When Deke invites Max and Caroline to join him and his parents for dinner at their home, Max is tongue-tied until his mother confides in her about her secret past.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The Wedding Cake Cake Cake
programme.desc: A girl who likes Max's cupcakes hires Max to make her wedding cake. Max who is still reeling from her break up, is hesitant to do it. She does it but the bride is constantly changing her mind about the cake and her marriage.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.sub-title: And The New Lease On Life
programme.desc: When Max and Caroline's rent controlled apartment's lease runs out they track down the original lease holder to stay with them for a day to renew the lease. But events turn south when the old man won't leave until he gets some action.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: With their season in a tailspin, Richmond try to right the ship against mighty Manchester City. Off the pitch, everyone faces their own setbacks.

programme.title: Ted Lasso
programme.desc: A friendly match takes the team to Amsterdam, where one night out unlocks truths for many.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman demonstrates how a battery works, answers spiraling questions such as "Who invented roller skates?" and explains why helium balloons fly away.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman shows how underwater tunnels are built; Art Burn cooks up glue you can make at home; and Beakman answers sizeable questions such as how big is a molecule.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: In response to a Massachusetts viewer's inquiry about bats, Beakman notes they are mammals, not birds, and their wings are actually hands with thin membranes of skin stretched between long fingers. Claiming they sleep upside down because it takes less energy, he goes on to explain that, contrary to popular myth, bats actually have good eyesight. Then, aided by Dr. Patricia Brown, Beakman explains how bats are able to hunt at night using sound to build a picture of their environment, or echolocation. During "Beakmania," Beakman notes that the longest living animal was a one hundred and forty?two-year?old turtle which died in 1918; that the most poisonous snake in the world is an Australian marine cobra; and that intense concentration can require as much energy as physical exercise. In the "Beakman Challenge," Lester is asked to break a ruler without holding onto it and using only a hammer. Although Lester doubts it can be done after trying the feat himself, Beakman shows that a large sheet of newspaper creates enough air resistance with its' surface area to hold the ruler firmly in place as it is being struck by the hammer. Asked how gliders fly without a motor, Beakman notes that they rely on converting potential energy into kinetic energy. After using a bowling ball and a swing to demonstrate these two types of energy, he explains that a tow plane transfers its potential energy to the glider by pulling it high into the air before the downward forces exerted by the Earth's gravity are converted into kinetic energy and forward motion by the glider's wings. Finally, after describing how their pilots use rising columns of hot air to stay aloft, Beakman notes that the highest recorded glider flight was to 49,009 feet ?? 10,000 feet higher than large commercial jetliners.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Beakman answers colorful questions such as why the sky is blue; Lester and Eliza enjoy a very special "Beakmoment" and Henry Ford drives home the description of how cars are made.

programme.title: Beakman's World
programme.desc: Pianos; maggots; illusions.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Dirty water, land, and air are a result of pollution. People are the only animals on Earth that make pollution. Garbage, burning fuel, chemicals, sewage, oil, and pesticides are all human-made things that make the Earth’s atmosphere, water, and soil unclean. Humans are even leaving trash in space, such as broken satellites, pieces of metal, paint from rocket skin, and even cameras and toothbrushes. Much of the junk people make and leave behind hurts plants, animals, you and me.

programme.title: Bill Nye: The Science Guy
programme.desc: Probability is a way to measure how likely it is that something will happen. Probabilities are predictions. They’re often just very careful guesses. When a scientist wants to calculate a probability, she or he gathers data and then uses the data to make her or his guess. Probabilities are between 100% (it’s definitely going to happen) and 0% (forget about it, pal, it’s not going to happen). Most things have a probability somewhere in between.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Singing: The town of Elmore becomes an all-singing, all-dancing mash up. Everyone has a song inside them, but do they really need to be heard? / The Best: No matter what Gumball does, Carmen thinks she knows better. Gumball attempts to gain the moral high ground but fails spectacularly. He decides that self-improvement isn't his style. The only option he has left is to bring Carmen down to his level.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons compete over who has the worst life.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Nicole finally gets a raise and an employee of the month plaque, Richard feels that he doesn't get enough credit for his work around the house. As a result, he goes on strike, causing the kids to revert to terrifying monsters.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Leslie's petals begin falling off, he feels like his good looks are fading, so Gumball and Darwin use everything they know about style and beauty to try and help him.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Darwin is delighted when they discover their old childhood toys, but soon realizes that some things are best left in the past.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons fear the worst when they are summoned to a Town Hall meeting, but are surprised when they're offered a new house - until they discover it's in a different state.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: The Wattersons will do whatever it takes to get into the first screening of the latest Stellar Odyssey sequel.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When the kids find out that Mom's life hasn't panned out as she'd hoped, they start taking on her chores, but they accidentally grab her bucket list instead of her to-do list.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: From police chases to the inside scoop on Daisy the Donkey, Channel 6 Elmore News has all the top local stories. Experienced newscaster Kip Schlezinger brings the latest news, sports and weather, even when there is absolutely nothing to report.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: When Anais is a newborn baby, Gumball and Darwin become jealous of their new sibling, and spark a baby war.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg gets sick, so Robin tries to use a shrink ray so he can go inside Cyborg and cure him, but the shrink ray doesn't make him small enough. Robin likes being small so much he decides to stay that way, but with Cyborg getting worse, the Titans modify the shrink ray and shrink themselves down enough to enter Cyborg.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Cyborg completely rebuilt the T-Car and takes it out on a road trip.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Raven’s father Trigon shows up for Thanksgiving dinner, Robin’s seating plan isn't the only thing that gets ruined.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Due to the Titans' laziness, Robin creates another team of himself to battle Brother Blood.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin wants to gain the power of whistling.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The Titans try to convince Beast Boy to clean his room, but they learn that there is more to garbage than just trash.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Nibor, Bizzaro World's Robin, arrives in Jump City, the team decides that they should watch him.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Crazy Day arrives, Raven tries to avoid being driven crazy by her fellow teammates.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: When Cyborg and Beast Boy teach the others to wolf down their food, the group must battle their own obesity.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: After transforming himself into 'Fleshy Guy', Cyborg discovers the delight and agony of being human.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc:
Annoyed by Stimpy's stupidity, Ren goes to live off as a hermit, and goes crazy after months of being alone. He is kicked out of the hermits union for having imaginary friends.
Segment: Untamed World: Field Guide - Stimpy, reprising his role as a nature show host from Untamed World, in the jungles of Miami, Florida.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: The chums seek refuge in a model home of the future which is awash with rare conveniences.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Stimpy has a friend in his face (like a tick), then Ren ends up with the friend's friend, which starts to annoy him to no end.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren goes into Stimpy's body to find a medical explanation to why he is so stupid.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren & Stimpy go to the Natural History Museum to see the skeletal remains of the mammoth Stimpysaurus. Wilbur Cobb tells them about the evolution of the great beast and says that even the meat-eating Renosaurus couldn't conquer the huge bumbling idiot.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Famished, Ren & Stimpy pretend to be long lost children of two Farmers.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Ren and Stimpy are two starving peasants in a fairy-tale world, so Stimpy tries to wrangle some wheels of magic singing cheese from the village idiot by besting him in a contest of stupidity.

programme.title: The Ren & Stimpy Show
programme.desc: Renwaldo sends Stimpleton on a food mission to trade their last chigger for some cheese. Stimpy meets a village idiot and trades his chigger for the magical golden singing cheeses.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys do a favor for Mr. Anderson (who they hope will die soon, and leave his money to them) by washing his dog Collette at the laundromat. Of course, they wind up in one of the dryers, too.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: The boys head to Maxi-Mart for some Friday night action; they meet a hot biker chick with larceny on her mind.

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.desc: Beavis and Butt-head are almost recruited into the Army by Sergeant Dick Leaky, a.k.a. "The Rooster."

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butthead - King Turd Collection - https://archive.org/details/beavis-and-butthead-king-turd-collection

programme.title: Beavis and Butt-Head
programme.sub-title: Beavis and Butthead - King Turd Collection - https://archive.org/details/beavis-and-butthead-king-turd-collection

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: The Edge (Wendy & Ethan) vs. Hatters (Allison & Chris)

programme.title: Double Dare
programme.sub-title: Shims (Shane & Kim) vs. Crazy Lightning Bolts (Erica & Chris)

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Batman's Hairdryer Fries Robin will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The studio's cameras keep breaking because of Ted's ugliness.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Mr. Clean Falls Into a Tar Pit will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross makes the cast wear sailor suits to prepare for a visit from the Senate's Committee to Clean Television. In the end, the inspector only wants to be sure the camera lenses are clean.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turkeys will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast wants to take martial arts lessons, but have to teach themselves because the show's budget can't afford an instructor.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Love Boat Becomes a Garbage Barge will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast talks about the major dangers to the environment.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
Fantasy Island Becomes a Volcano will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
Ross has a box that can grant anyone's wishes. He plans to give it to the producer's daughter. The rest of the cast has all their dreams come true.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
The Days of Our Lives will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The budget for the show has been cut so it must be filmed in black and white. All the kids go into a decolorization machine. When the producers realize that color shows are more profitable the kids can't switch back. In their attempts to overcome the glitch, they turn the show into a silent movie at the end.

programme.title: You Can't Do That on Television
programme.desc:
George Bush Shoots the Wrong Quail will not be seen at this time so that we may present...
The cast looks at their mistakes, and Ross has to confront some of his errors.

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Bush/Cheney supporters take on the Kerry/Edwards supporters in this special episode's battle between President Bush and Senator Kerry.
Games include: Sinkers and Floaters; Pie in the Sky; Boulder Dash; Character Assassination

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
People who strike oil take on people who give face lifts in this MXC competition.
Games include: Roulective Surgery; Rotating Surfboards of Death; Dash to Death; Mudd Butlers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Hollywood's film industry challenges a group with phobias on this edition of MXC. Also, Kenny teams up with Anne Heche to make a new movie, which Kenny treats like an adult movie.
Games include: Get a Piece; Saddle Sores; Pole Riders; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The people who make love take on the people who own firearms. Make sure to keep your guns cocked!
Games include: Great Holes of Glory; Dope on a Rope; Skidmarkers; Wall Buggers

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
The footlockers take on the game boys (and girls) in this first episode of 2005. Kenny and Vic also try out some new athletic footwear.
Games include: Spare Me; Sinkers and Floaters; Chum in the Mouth; Boulder Dash

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
It's the septic sluggers vs. TV pluggers as waste management takes on advertising. Vic and Kenny discuss the significant role of advertising in American culture by exploring the ad campaign for Cocoa Puffs.
Games include: Bagel Bumpers; Sperm Wheelers; Tumbling Dominoes of Doom; Rotating Surfboard of Death

programme.title: MXC
programme.desc:
Only MXC can bring together bakers and malcontents for an all-out war.
Games include: Bust-a-Nut; Bird Droppings; Irritable Bowl Syndrome; Log Drop

programme.title: MXC
programme.sub-title: Mall Workers vs. The Telephone Company
programme.desc:
MXC creates a war between those who work at the mall and the telephone industry. The Captain revels in memories of better days spent at the mall and Guy crank calls employees who work the emergency line.
Games include: Eat Shitake; Circle Jerkers; In and Outhouse; Dope on a Rope

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: When Daria agrees to help Trent find a birthday present for Jane, they somehow end up at a piercing parlor offering a two-for-one special. Hmm.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Daria is assigned to write a short story turning people she knows into fictional characters. Like they're not unbelievable enough already.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The threat of a Lawndale hurricane causes people to start singing and dancing!

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: An obstruction by her glasses during a driving lesson causes Daria to contemplate trading them in for contact lenses. The trouble is, she fears that by doing so she will be viewed as being just as vain and superficial as the rest of her vapid fellow classmates.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The new spirit of "volunteerism" is mandatory at Lawndale High. While reading to senior citizens, Daria finds that her unenthusiastic tone makes her just as unpopular at the nursing home as at school.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: A fantasy show wherein Daria and Jane have to convince Christmas, Halloween and Guy Fawkes Day to return to Holiday Island.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: Quinn gets roped into organizing the school dance; she fobs the job off on Jane.

programme.title: Daria
programme.desc: The publisher of a "Savvy"-like teen magazine comes to Lawndale to hang out with Daria.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Great Britain is in complete chaos as every cup of tea has turned into low grade dishwater. Baron Greenback is collecting the remaining tea supply by tractor ray beam to his teapot shaped satellite, the TB1.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Unpacking from a holiday in Mexico, Penfold discovers El Loco hiding in his suitcase. El Loco gets loose and goes on a crime spree in London.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.sub-title: Have You Fled From Any Good Books Lately?
programme.desc: Penfold receives an intergalactic delivery: a free gift from the Cosmos book of the light year club. It's the 'Cowards Pop-up book of Slobbering Monsters by I.L. Scaremstiff. Once he opens it, J.J. Quark unleashes a remote animation ray from space to bring the monsters to life.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K has had word that Greenback knows the location of the long lost amulet of Egganofice, a medallion fashioned by an ancient Egyptian sorcerer in 5002 B.C. which possesses unlimited mysterious powers. In truth, Greenback plans to let Danger Mouse do the hard work for him and grab the amulet once DM finds it for him.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Professor Squackencluck is lost on an island in the China sea and feels the need to be rescued. Dangermouse deduces that the island in question must be Combaktu and sets off on a daring mission, accompanied by Penfold of course. On arrival, a tricky Witch doctor manages to split our heroes up. While DM is matching wits with the doctor, Penfold finds himself in the clutches of an Uran Utang.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold swallows a new sort of energy pill invented by Professor Squackencluck and goes all superhero on his foes. However, it hinders Danger Mouse even more than usual in his search for a stray messenger pigeon.

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Radio Control Plane - Square On Balloon - Armadillo Patient - Folded Paper Flips - Vectrex Drawing - Oldest Living Thing - Touch - Secret Inks - Fireproof Money

programme.title: Mr. Wizard's World
programme.desc: - Plastic Hot Air Balloon - Glue Powered Bugs - Apple Flower - Binary Number System - Lung's Capacity - Unbreakable Container - Spoon Into Glass - Suspended Book
